response.justification: Wei Chen is a versatile full-stack and systems engineer with broad experience, but fails to meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The job requires 8 years of C++ experience; Chen's entire C++ exposure amounts to roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not appear in the skills section at all. There is no evidence of technical leadership in cloud compute, DevOps, or large-scale infrastructure platforms, and no formal technical lead role is listed. The candidate's demonstrated strengths in web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang development are irrelevant to the role's core technology requirements.
strengths: Exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks
Polyglot background with some DevOps exposure (Docker, Kubernetes, GitLab CI, AWS CDK) showing operational breadth
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months in 2014) versus the 8-year minimum requirement; C++ is absent from the skills list entirely
No technical leadership role in cloud compute, infrastructure, or platform engineering; leadership evidence is limited to community workshops and an open-source side project
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is orthogonal to the role's core requirements of C++ and cloud compute platform engineering
